data.title: Microservices Infrastructure
data.description: Adobe uses a microservices architecture to manage its wide array of services.
data.technical_spec: Decoupled services with API communication handled via REST and GraphQL.

data.title: Cloud-Native Deployment
data.description: Many of Adobe's services are deployed in a cloud-native fashion to enhance scalability and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes AWS and Azure for elastic scaling and global distribution.

data.title: Reactive Event-Driven Model
data.description: The system operates on event-driven architecture to handle asynchronous data flow efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Kafka and RabbitMQ are leveraged for queue management across services.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Adobe employs CDNs to deliver content globally with optimized latency and faster load times.
data.technical_spec: Partnerships with Akamai and CloudFront for edge cache and security.

data.title: Serverless Functions
data.description: Adobe integrates serverless functions to perform backend operations without managing infrastructure.
data.technical_spec: AWS Lambda functions orchestrated for operations automation and batch processing workflows.
